observations_and_minor_notes.note: A8 was correctly marked evaluated_this_turn=False at OOS. Cost-stress on OOS would require a fresh authorization (option_3). The IS cost-stress matrix already validated structural cost-robustness across S0-S3. Not a defect; correctly scoped.
observations_and_minor_notes.topic: A8 cost-stress not run at OOS

observations_and_minor_notes.note: K10 was committed (4ddaa84e) at 00:18 UTC, P10 at 15231cb at 00:33 UTC. K10 PASS was known before P10 ran. However, K10 seal does not appear in P10/P11 inherited_seals list. This is a chain-of-custody gap (minor) but not a substantive defect: K10 was independently sealed, verdict is in the record, and the chain remains auditable via separate commit references.

OOS window ends 2025-12-31; today is 2026-05-27. Additional ~5 months of data would theoretically be available. Extending the OOS window would be option_2 (longer_oos_window) requiring fresh authorization. Cutoff frozen in the OOS driver constants at P3.6 BUILD.
observations_and_minor_notes.topic: OOS window cutoff vs current date

observations_and_minor_notes.note: OOS 17.7 trades/year vs IS 20 trades/year (ratio 0.88) demonstrates the s8-D1 sizing-fix lesson was applied correctly. Sizing is NOT the binding constraint here, unlike s8-D1. The binding constraint is OOS window length.

The candidate that looked structurally strongest IS-side (READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST, K12 clear, K10 clear, all safety gates clear) was correctly PARKED on OOS due to K9. Framework refused to overinterpret a directionally-consistent OOS or loosen thresholds. This is the framework working as intended.

DR9 (cross-symbol data continuity gt 5 missing) was the rule that killed s10-D1 IS. At OOS, DR9 wasn't explicitly re-evaluated, but the OOS cache passed EXPECTED_CACHE_BYTES check (36 files per root with exact byte counts). Structural pre-check serves as implicit DR9 enforcement.

common_lessons: Both candidates produced clean IS verdicts
Both candidates were directionally consistent in OOS
Both candidates parked under different binding-constraint roots
Both candidates explicitly remained BLOCKED_AT_6_GATES with respect to live promotion
Neither candidate produced a profitable proven claim
comparison_vs_s8_d1_precedent.s10_d2_reason: OOS sample insufficient (K9 fired at 53 < 100 trades over 3 years). The s10-D2 sizing fix to $500,000 succeeded in producing comparable per-year trade density; the binding constraint was OOS WINDOW LENGTH, not sizing.
comparison_vs_s8_d1_precedent.s8_d1_reason: Capital-vs-contract-size mismatch in OOS (sizing produced 0 contracts for most signals)

decision_logic_8_steps: P6 IS was clean enough to advance to cost-stress (200 trades, all safety zero, 0 K-gates fired)
P6.5 cost-stress matrix was clean enough to advance to OOS
P7 correctly advanced to OOS gate
P10 OOS produced directionally-consistent metrics but K9 fired (53 < 100)
Safety counters remained zero across IS + 5 cost tiers + OOS
OOS sample was structurally limited by available 3-year window vs 10-year IS
Positive OOS direction is encouraging but is NOT proof
Conservative lifecycle decision is PARK -- not ADVANCE, not KILL, not PROMOTE

All anchor seals (Tier-N spec, plan-lock, phase-2 plan) inherited byte-equivalent through every phase
